_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
specialty: General Surgery, Pediatric Surgery, Genitourinary Surgery
clinical_indications: Clinical presentation of epididymal nodule varies from silent incidental discovery (asymptomatic) to multiple episodes of inguino-scrotal pain
Most non-neoplastic lesions are managed conservatively
Symptomatic nodules are treated by surgical excision

clinical_thresholds.note: Symptomatic nodules are treated by surgical excision

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure and planned line of management

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, indication for procedure?
Did clinical evaluation and Scrotal ultrasound/FNAC confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical notes missing or incomplete, lacking detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, or indication for procedure
Diagnosis not confirmed by clinical evaluation or Scrotal ultrasound/FNAC
Nodule not documented as symptomatic to justify surgical excision
Scrotal ultrasound not submitted at pre-authorization
Detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details missing or incomplete
Detailed procedure / Operative Notes not available
Discharge summary with follow-up advice not provided

clinical_key_pointers: Most of the cases are non-neoplastic lesions
The clinical presentation of epididymal nodule varies from silent incidental discovery (asymptomatic) to multiple episodes of inguino-scrotal pain
Fine-needle aspiration cytology (FNAC) is useful in diagnosis and guide management strategies of patients with epididymal nodules
Most non-neoplastic lesions are managed conservatively. Symptomatic nodules are treated by surgical excision
surgical_options: Symptomatic nodules are treated by surgical excision
